threats.title: Weak Password Policy Leads to Credential Theft
threats.description: The system lacks information about password complexity requirements or enforcement. This allows an attacker to create weak passwords, potentially leading to successful credential theft through brute-force or dictionary attacks.  Without multi-factor authentication, compromised credentials provide immediate access.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attack, credential stuffing, dictionary attack targeting username/password combination.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (minimum length, character requirements, regular password changes).
Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.
Utilize rate limiting and account lockout mechanisms to mitigate brute-force attacks.

threats.title: SQL Injection via Untrusted Input
threats.description: The system’s database interaction lacks explicit input validation and sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., search boxes, forms) to manipulate the database, potentially leading to data breaches, data modification, or even complete system takeover.
threats.attack_vector: User input directly used in SQL queries without proper escaping or parameterized queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization to restrict acceptable input characters and formats.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
threats.description: The system’s handling of user-supplied data, particularly within web pages, is not properly sanitized. This allows an attacker to inject malicious JavaScript code into a website, which can then be executed by other users’ browsers, potentially stealing cookies, redirecting users to malicious sites, or defacing the website.
threats.attack_vector: Injection of malicious JavaScript code into input fields or URLs.
mitigations: Implement output encoding to properly escape special characters before displaying data in web pages.
Use a Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict the sources from which the browser can load resources.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious requests.

threats.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
threats.description: Without proper authorization checks, users can potentially access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to access by manipulating identifiers (e.g., database keys, file paths) directly in URLs or requests. This could allow a user to gain administrative privileges or access sensitive data.
threats.attack_vector: Direct manipulation of identifiers in URLs or requests to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization controls to verify user permissions before granting access to resources.
Use indirect object references (e.g., UUIDs) instead of direct identifiers.
Enforce the principle of least privilege – grant users only the minimum necessary permissions.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The system is vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks if it processes large or malformed inputs that consume excessive server resources (CPU, memory, bandwidth). This can lead to the system becoming unresponsive or crashing.
threats.attack_vector: Sending crafted requests with oversized payloads, complex queries, or requests that trigger resource-intensive operations.
mitigations: Implement input validation and sanitization to limit the size and complexity of user-supplied data.
Rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single IP address.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,database threat

rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Password Policy Leads to Credential Theft
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection via Untrusted Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
